title: buddleja cordata subsp. tomentella
intro: Buddleja cordata subsp. tomentella, also known as the Himalayan or silver-leaved Buddleja, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and polyphenols. The health benefits of Buddleja cordata subsp. tomentella include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, while also being used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
